statement.en: Boundary Geometry: From Cosmic Expansion to Wave Collapse
statement.summary: Two unresolved problems remain central in modern physics: cosmic acceleration at macroscopic scale and quantum state reduction at microscopic scale. These are usually treated in separate frameworks and at the level of three-dimensional bulk dynamics. A different possibility is considered here. Both are examined in terms of two-dimensional boundary geometry through a scale-invariant master relation. Information transfer is written as a boundary-crossing process governed by geometric scale, transition frequency, and a dimensionless transmission efficiency. The macroscopic limit is associated with horizon-scale saturation, whereas the microscopic limit is associated with radiative extraction through a finite decoupling surface. reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper proposes speculative unification framework (cosmic expansion + wave collapse via boundary geometry) not yet peer-reviewed or empirically validated. Claims are theoretically ambitious but lack supporting evidence, experimental predictions, or mathematical proof. Status: preprint-level hypothesis requiring validation.
